# Phase 30 â€” Sourcemap hunt (resolves [27.D1])

## Why

`pnpm test` exited 1 on Windows even with zero failing tests, because vitest's stack-trace symbolicator threw on a malformed inline sourcemap somewhere in the dep graph. Logged as `[27.D1]`. This phase finds the actual file and makes the runner tolerant.

## What was happening

`lib/template-seed.test.ts` spawns `npx tsx prisma/seed.ts` and the seed throws `ENOENT` because `templates/` is gitignored and never populated on this Windows box. Vitest catches the error from the child stack and tries to symbolicate the frame pointing into `node_modules/.pnpm/tsx@4.21.0/node_modules/tsx/dist/register-D46fvsV_.cjs`. That file contains the literal string `sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;base64,` followed by a backtick â€” it's tsx's own code that *generates* sourcemap comments at runtime. `convert-source-map`'s comment regex matches that literal as if it were a real inline sourcemap, base64-decodes the following JS source, and JSON.parses it, throwing `Unexpected token 'ďż˝'`. That throw is unhandled inside vitest's stack-trace processor and breaks the exit code.

Not a tsx bug. Not a sourcemap-content bug. A false-positive match in a regex inside a bundled tool.

## What ships

1. **Tracked pnpm patch on `@vitest/utils@4.1.2`** â€” wraps `extractSourcemapFromFile` in a try/catch and returns `undefined` on parse failure. Missing one source-map for a vendored bundle is harmless; breaking the test runner exit code is not. Committed via `pnpm patch` â†’ `patches/@vitest__utils@4.1.2.patch` + `pnpmPatchedDependencies` entry in `package.json`. Survives `pnpm install` cleanly.
2. **`lib/template-seed.test.ts` skips when `templates/` is missing.** Previously the file errored at `beforeAll`. Now it conditionally `describe.skip`s the suite and the `afterAll` short-circuits â€” no spurious failures on fresh-checkout machines without populated templates.

## Acceptance criteria â†’ tests

| # | Criterion | Test |
|---|-----------|------|
| 1 | Full suite exits 0 on Windows | manual: `pnpm test ; echo $?` returns 0 |
| 2 | template-seed test skips cleanly when default template is missing | run on this Windows box (templates/ absent) â†’ 5 skipped, 0 errors |
| 3 | template-seed test runs when templates are present | logic preserved; on a populated machine, `describeIfTemplates === describe` |
| 4 | Patch survives `pnpm install` | wired via `pnpmPatchedDependencies` |

## Out of scope

- Upstreaming the fix to vitest. The patch is a one-line try/catch â€” easy to drop if vitest publishes a more permissive `extractSourcemapFromFile`.
- Populating `templates/` on this Windows box. That's a per-machine setup task; the test now degrades gracefully instead of erroring.
